Years of meticulous surveillance, including hacked traffic cameras and disrupted phone networks, provided crucial intelligence. This enabled a precise strike during a rare meeting, marking a significant escalation driven by political decisions and advanced technological capabilities.

title: From CAA to Kashmir: Why India is silent over Khamenei's demise
description: India's measured response to Iran's supreme leader's assassination, focusing on regional de-escalation, has sparked domestic criticism. While condemning Iranian strikes on Gulf allies, New Delhi has avoided direct condemnation of the killing, prioritizing national interests like energy security and diaspora welfare. This stance contrasts with past criticisms of Iran's internal affairs.

title: From Khamenei's compound to nuclear site: Satellite images show aftermath of US-Israel strikes
description: High-resolution satellite images reveal extensive destruction across Iran following US-Israeli strikes on nuclear, military, and leadership sites. Khamenei was killed in one of the initial strikes on Saturday.

title: TN double murder: 2 hacked to death outside tea shop; 5 injured, heavy police deployed
description: Tragedy struck Perumpathu village as a brutal attack left two dead, including a guest worker, and five injured. A gang reportedly hacked seven individuals outside a tea shop late Monday night.  The victims, identified as Trinath Kata and John Mark, died instantly.  Villagers protested, demanding swift arrests. Police have secured six individuals as investigations continue amidst a tense atmosphere.

title: Netflix CEO: WB deal will force Paramount CEO David Ellison to layoff thousands
description: Netflix's Ted Sarandos revealed the company bowed out of the Warner Bros. Discovery bidding war, deeming Paramount's debt-laden offer too risky. He warned of significant layoffs and reduced production at the rival studio. Despite the loss, Netflix will invest its saved funds into its core business and expand its theatrical presence.

title: Tech employees across America send open letter to Pentagon on Anthropic
description: Hundreds of tech employees have signed an open letter urging the Department of War and Congress to withdraw Anthropic's "supply chain risk" designation. This action followed a failed contract negotiation, leading to a presidential directive to cease using Anthropic's technology. The letter argues this precedent punishes American innovation and urges a resolution through commercial channels.

title: Sam Altman says he can't speak for the Pentagon, but Anthropic seemed more focused on controls
description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman suggested Anthropic's stricter contractual demands may have led to their failed Pentagon deal. While OpenAI secured a contract, Anthropic was declared a national security risk. Altman emphasized OpenAI's reliance on technical safeguards over contract language for safety in government deployments.
